title: core: pad() and unpad() payload padding utilities
description:
What: Add pad() and unpad() functions to core/src/messages.rs for length-prefix bucket padding of opaque payload bytes.

Algorithm:
1. Add pub fn pad(plaintext: &[u8]) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<u8>>:
   a. If plaintext.len() > 4096: return Err(anyhow!("payload too large: {} bytes (max 4096)", plaintext.len()))
   b. Choose bucket: match plaintext.len() { 0..=256 => 256, 257..=512 => 512, 513..=1024 => 1024, _ => 4096 }
   c. Encode original_len as u32 little-endian: 4 bytes
   d. Build output: original_len_bytes (4) + plaintext (N) + zero bytes (bucket - 4 - N)
   e. Return Ok(output)

2. Add pub fn unpad(padded: &[u8]) -> anyhow::Result<Vec<u8>>:
   a. If padded.len() < 4: return Err(anyhow!("padded message too short"))
   b. Read original_len = u32::from_le_bytes(padded[0..4]) as usize
   c. If original_len > padded.len() - 4: return Err(anyhow!("length prefix {} exceeds message bounds", original_len))
   d. Return Ok(padded[4..4+original_len].to_vec())

3. Add unit tests in #[cfg(test)] mod tests:
   - pad_empty: pad(&[]) → 256-byte output with len prefix 0x00000000 then 252 zero bytes
   - pad_256_boundary: pad(&[0u8; 256]) → 260-byte output with len 0x00010000 then 0 padding bytes — WAIT this is wrong. 256 bytes + 4 byte prefix = 260 bytes. Bucket 256 is too small. Actually bucket must hold prefix + data. So bucket should be chosen based on prefix+data size: choose smallest bucket >= 4 + plaintext.len().
   - pad_1024_max: pad(&[1u8; 1024]) → 1028 bytes? No... Let me reconsider.

REVISED ALGORITHM for bucket selection:
- total = 4 + plaintext.len()  (4 bytes for length prefix + data)
- bucket = smallest B in {260, 516, 1028, 4100} where B >= total
  OR simpler: choose smallest B in {256, 512, 1024, 4096} where B >= 4 + plaintext.len()
  - This means max payload size is 4096 - 4 = 4092 bytes
  - Reject if plaintext.len() > 4092

Revised algorithm:
1. pad(plaintext):
   a. If plaintext.len() > 4092: Err (4092 = 4096 - 4 byte prefix)
   b. total_needed = 4 + plaintext.len()
   c. bucket = first B in [256, 512, 1024, 4096] where B >= total_needed
   d. output = u32le(plaintext.len()) + plaintext + zeros(bucket - total_needed)
   e. assert output.len() == bucket
   f. Return Ok(output)

2. unpad(padded):
   a. if padded.len() < 4: Err
   b. original_len = u32le(padded[0..4])
   c. if 4 + original_len > padded.len(): Err
   d. Return Ok(padded[4..4+original_len].to_vec())

Tests (oracle = manual byte computation, independent of implementation):
- pad_empty: input = [], expected output = [0,0,0,0] + [0u8;252] (256 bytes total)
- pad_1_byte: input = [0x42], expected = [1,0,0,0, 0x42, 0,0,...(251 zeros)] = 256 bytes
- pad_252_bytes: input = [1u8;252], expected = [252,0,0,0] + [1u8;252] = 256 bytes (exact bucket)
- pad_253_bytes: input = [1u8;253], expected = [253,0,0,0] + [1u8;253] + [0u8;3] = 260 bytes? No wait, 4+253=257, next bucket is 512. So 512 bytes total.
- pad_too_large: input = [0u8;4093], expected = Err
- unpad_roundtrip: unpad(pad(x)?) == x for x in {[], [42u8], [1u8;252], [1u8;253]}
- unpad_too_short: unpad(&[0,0,0]) = Err
- unpad_length_overflow: unpad(&[255,255,255,255,0,0,0,0]) = Err (claims 4GB but only 4 bytes after prefix)

Defensive rules:
- Never unwrap() in pad/unpad — all error paths return Err
- Input is untrusted (payload bytes come from client-controlled message content)
- Do not log the payload bytes (may contain user message content)

Team: standard R+I+T
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-core passes; pad_empty, pad_1_byte, unpad_roundtrip, unpad_too_short tests all pass; clippy -D warnings clean
